applicability_notes: Elementwise binary subtract with implicit broadcasting. Observed idioms: (1) mean-centering `x - mean(x, dim, keepdim=True)` in stats-pooling / norm blocks; (2) self-subtraction `subtract(input_ids, input_ids)` to materialize a zeros tensor matching input shape/dtype for default token_type_ids.

placement_observations.size_descriptor: generic rand bf16 shapes incl. broadcast shapes
placement_observations.source: unit test: ttnn.subtract(input_tensor_a, input_tensor_b, memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG, use_legacy=None)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: [N, L, C] hidden states minus broadcast [N, 1, C] mean (stats-pooling activation regime)
placement_observations.source: call site: centered = ttnn.subtract(hidden_tt, mean, memory_config=ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG) in std-pooling branch
call site: ttnn.subtract(x_nlc, mean, memory_config=mc) within mean/std pooling chain feeding ttnn.concat with same mc

placement_observations.size_descriptor: [N, 1, C] reduced statistic (mean / weighted_mean), broadcast over dim=1
placement_observations.source: call site: ttnn.subtract(x_nlc, weighted_mean, memory_config=mc) — statistic produced by ttnn.mean/ttnn.sum keepdim=True
